title: Chemical Splash to Skin or Eyes
summary: Prioritize decontamination, isolate the source, and activate medical escalation early.
trigger_signs: Chemical enters eyes, face, or exposed skin
Burning pain, redness, blurred vision, or damaged gloves/clothing
immediate_actions: Stop the operation and call for nearby support immediately.
Use eyewash or emergency shower continuously according to local SOP.
Remove contaminated gloves, coat, or face shield while flushing.
Bring the SDS or chemical label information for handoff.
forbidden: Do not delay flushing while searching for paperwork.
Do not neutralize on the body unless the local SOP explicitly requires it.
Do not let the exposed person continue working.
escalation: Notify supervisor and institutional emergency contact.
Seek medical evaluation for eye exposure, persistent pain, or unknown chemicals.

title: Small Laboratory Fire or Ignition
summary: Only fight a small controllable fire if trained and exit remains clear; otherwise evacuate immediately.
trigger_signs: Visible flame, burning solvent, electrical smoke, or overheated equipment
Rapid heat rise, ignition near flammables, or failed heating control
immediate_actions: Stop the process if safe, isolate gas/power, and warn nearby people.
Use the correct extinguisher only if the fire is small and you are trained.
If fire is not controlled immediately, evacuate and close the door behind you.
Report the location and material involved to emergency responders.
forbidden: Do not fight a growing fire alone.
Do not use the wrong extinguisher for solvent, metal, or electrical fire.
ppe: Standard lab PPE for evacuation support
Thermal or specialized PPE only for trained responders
escalation: Trigger the institutional fire alarm and emergency call chain.
Record involved chemicals, gases, and cylinders for responder briefing.

title: Electrical Shock or Energized Equipment Incident
summary: Cut power first, avoid direct contact with the victim, and escalate medical support immediately.
trigger_signs: Sparks, exposed conductors, damaged insulation, or burning smell
immediate_actions: Isolate power using emergency stop, breaker, or unplug only if safe.
Use a non-conductive object to separate the victim from the source if still energized.
Call emergency medical support and monitor breathing until responders arrive.
Secure the area and preserve the equipment state for incident review.
forbidden: Do not touch the victim directly before power is isolated.
Do not re-energize or test the device after the event.
Do not ignore minor shock symptoms.
escalation: Immediate medical evaluation is required after electrical exposure.
Notify equipment owner and safety manager before restart or repair.

title: Chemical Spill or Leak
summary: Assess size and toxicity quickly, isolate the zone, and use the spill SOP instead of improvised cleanup.
trigger_signs: Liquid on bench or floor, broken container, spreading odor, or visible vapor
Unknown substance outside containment or damaged transport container
immediate_actions: Stop nearby work, isolate the area, and identify the material if possible.
Use spill kit, absorbent, or containment tools only if the spill is within your training level.
Increase local ventilation if the SOP allows and exposure risk is manageable.
Package contaminated absorbents and waste according to the waste route.
forbidden: Do not touch unknown material with bare hands.
Do not wash incompatible chemicals into the drain without approval.
Do not continue nearby ignition or heat-generating work.
ppe: Respiratory protection if required by SOP
escalation: Escalate immediately for large, unknown, toxic, or highly volatile spills.
Report the material, quantity, and exposure status to the supervisor and safety contact.
